content:
@Wedson Almeida Filho am working on abstractions for struct radix_tree and I was surprised that ForeignOwnable::borrow_mut() does not capture a lifetime for the borrow. This is odd to me, since ForeignOwnable::borrow() does capture lifetime. I got around it by wrapping the ScopeGuard in a struct that takes a generic lifetime. But I wonder if maybe we should change ForeignOwnable? Or is it intentionally designed the way it is?

RadixTree is here: https://github.com/metaspace/linux/commit/353fd5dc2407ab6d81a8fc862fd40eb9a473b693
